pages.intro: You made it through 12 activities — that's a LOT of new vocabulary! This page reviews all 12 words you learned. Try to remember them BEFORE flipping back to the activities!

section_1.header: Match the Word to the Definition
section_1.instruction: Draw a line from each word on the LEFT to its definition on the RIGHT.

definitions_shuffled.text: A rule that predicts what comes next in a sequence
A step-by-step set of instructions for solving a problem
A mistake in code that makes it do the wrong thing
To find a path from one place to another
The order in which steps happen
To plan and create something from scratch
A Yes/No question that decides what happens next
To do the same thing again and again
A block of code that runs over and over
When something is INSIDE another thing of the same type
To put things in order (like smallest to largest)

section_2.instruction: Find 8 vocabulary words hidden in the grid! Words can go across, down, or diagonally.
section_2.generation_instruction: Generate the grid programmatically — see Kimi instruction. Do not use a hardcoded grid.

pages.intro: You've completed the whole workbook! Try this 10-question quiz to see how much stuck. Don't peek at earlier pages — trust yourself!

rows.label: Nice Try — Review a few activities
No worries — learning is a loop!

questions.question: Which of these is an algorithm?
When steps are done in the WRONG _______, the algorithm fails.
Which of these is a LOOP?

options.text: Wake up, brush teeth, eat breakfast — done!

questions.question: Write a loop for "do 10 jumping jacks" in the format: REPEAT "___" N TIMES
A conditional starts with which word?
A nested conditional means putting one Yes/No question INSIDE another.
If a robot's program is missing a step, that's called a:

options.text: The biggest number with the smallest number
Two adjacent items and swapping if wrong
Every item with every other item at the same time

questions.question: The sequence 2, 4, 6, 8, 10 is what type of pattern?
Name ONE tool you learned in this workbook (loop, conditional, sort, etc.):
questions.correct: any workbook tool (algorithm, sequence, loop, conditional, sorting, debugging, pattern recognition, grid navigation)

pages.closing_callout: How did you do? Every question you got right proves you're thinking like a computer scientist!
